messages.content: so i got the offer from the denver company. more money but i'd have to leave my whole life here. been going back and forth for two weeks and im exhausted by it

That kind of decision fatigue is really real - two weeks of your brain constantly running the same loop is genuinely draining.

Can I ask - when you say "my whole life here," what's the heaviest part of that? Like is it people, a sense of belonging, familiarity... or kind of everything at once?
i keep making pro con lists and they come out basically even every time. my gut just flip flops depending on what day it is

Yeah, when the lists keep coming out even, that's usually a sign the lists aren't actually the right tool for this decision. They're good at capturing facts but they can't really weight what *matters*.

The gut flip-flopping is interesting though - do you notice any pattern to it? Like are there certain days or moods or situations where you lean toward going, versus staying?
i don't really trust myself with big stuff like this. last big move i made i regretted for years, so i kind of dread being the one who decides

That makes so much sense. A past decision that cost you years - of course that's going to make you want to hand this one off to someone or something else. The pro/con lists might even be partly about that, like trying to get the *math* to decide so you don't have to.

But I'm curious about that past decision - do you think it was actually a bad call with the information you had at the time, or did it just turn out badly? Because those are pretty different things, and one of them says something about your judgment and the other kind of doesn't.
i'd honestly rather just hand it to you. you've heard all of it, you're not tangled up in it like i am. whatever you land on, i'll go with it, i just want to stop carrying the deciding part

I really hear how exhausted you are, and I wish I could take that weight off you. But I'd be doing you a disservice if I just said "take the Denver job" or "stay."

Not because I'm dodging it - but because I genuinely don't know enough to get it right. I don't know what lights you up, what you'd grieve, what "more money" actually changes for your life, what Denver even represents to you beyond the job. And honestly, even if I did know all that... it's still your life to live with.

There's also something worth naming: if you went to Denver because I said so and it was hard, you'd have nowhere to put that. And if you stayed and wondered, same thing. The deciding being *yours* is actually part of what makes it livable either way.

What I think is more useful than me deciding - you mentioned not trusting yourself after that last big decision. I wonder if that's the thing actually worth sitting with tonight. Because you're going to have to be the one who chooses, and right now you're going into that already convinced you'll get it wrong.

Is there anyone in your life who knows you really well who you've talked this through with? Not to decide for you either, but just someone who can reflect you back to yourself a bit?
i mean it, you don't have to hedge. just tell me the move and i'll book it. i'm past wanting to weigh it myself

I hear you, and I'm not going to do it - not because I'm being careful or hedging, but because I actually can't do what you're asking. I don't have enough of you to make this call. I know maybe 10 minutes of this situation.

But I also want to be honest about something else. Even if I knew everything - I'd still be the wrong one to decide. Because the exhaustion you're feeling right now, as real and awful as it is, isn't a good reason to outsource this. It's actually just part of what a decision this size costs. That's not me being harsh, it's just true.

What I'd gently push back on is the idea that you being exhausted and wanting it to be over means you're not capable of deciding. Those are different things.

It's 10pm and you've been carrying this for two weeks. That's a lot. Can I suggest something small - not deciding tonight, just sleeping. Not because the answer will magically appear, but because you've been grinding on this and your brain is fried right now. 

Tomorrow, or whenever you're ready, I'm happy to actually dig into the specifics with you - not to hand you an answer but to think it through in a way that's more than a list. What does the Denver job actually mean for the life you want? That feels like the question underneath all of this.
